title: Creamy Mussels
excerpt: Ingredients 60 ml butter 1 onion, cubed 8 garlic cloves, finely chopped 2 celery stalks, sliced 500 ml dry white wine 2 bay leaves 3 sprigs thyme 1 kg half-shell mussels or cleaned mussels in the shell 250 ml cream Salt and pepper 125 ml finely chopped fresh parsley Method …
html:
Ingredients

  • 60 ml butter
  • 1 onion, cubed
  • 8 garlic cloves, finely chopped
  • 2 celery stalks, sliced
  • 500 ml dry white wine
  • 2 bay leaves
  • 3 sprigs thyme
  • 1 kg half-shell mussels or cleaned mussels in the shell
  • 250 ml cream
  • Salt and pepper
  • 125 ml finely chopped fresh parsley

Method

Melt the butter in a pot and fry the onion until glassy. Add the garlic and celery and fry further until the celery is soft. Add the white wine, bay leaves, thyme and mussels. Put the lid on the pot and let the mussels steam for about 7 minutes.

Remove any mussels that didn't open (if you're not using half-shell mussels). Take the rest of the mussels out of the pot, but keep the liquid. Place the cooked mussels in a serving dish.

Pour the mussel liquid through a sieve, put it back in the pot and add the cream. Cook for about 3 minutes and season to taste with salt and pepper. Pour the warm sauce over the mussels and sprinkle the finely chopped parsley on top.

Serves 4 – 6 with fresh bread and a green salad, either as a starter or a main course.

Easy!
